prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a nurp has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the nurp-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a nurp, a vask, and a rast. The nurp is underneath the vask. The vask is to-the-left-of the rast. The nurp has a purple button on top, a blue button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The vask has a blue button on top and a purple button on bottom. The rast has a yellow button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the nurp activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a yib, a trob, and a arn. The trob is underneath the yib. The yib is to-the-left-of the arn. The yib has a purple button on top, a purple button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The trob has a purple button on top, a blue button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The arn has a yellow button on top and a blue button on bottom. Notably, the yib's purple button glows softly when anyone walks by.

Which object in the perception scenario is the nurp-analog, and which button activates it?
